next_steps: Bronchoalveolar lavage with cell count, Gram stain, fungal/bacterial/mycobacterial cultures, and specific PCR for Strongyloides and PJP
Skin biopsy of the abdominal purpuric lesions
Blood cultures with prolonged incubation for fastidious organisms

differential_diagnoses: Gram-negative sepsis with secondary purpura fulminans
next_steps: Perform diagnostic skin biopsy of purpuric lesions for histology and culture
Obtain bronchial washings and sputum for Gram stain, fungal smear, and Strongyloides larvae (Baermann technique)
Perform repeat serum cortisol and ACTH stimulation test
Initiate empiric ivermectin coverage for potential Strongyloides hyperinfection
Collect blood cultures with prolonged incubation for fastidious organisms
Consider abdominal CT with contrast to evaluate for bowel perforation, ischemia, or abscess
Consult infectious disease and transplant medicine for consideration of empiric pathogen-directed therapy
